title: Pre-Registered Trial Protocol Fluconazole vs. Placebo in Adult Irritable Bowel Syndrome with Adaptive Mycobiome Enrichment Analysis
author: Craddock, Jim
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This document is a pre-registered clinical trial protocol for a Phase 2 randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial of oral fluconazole versus placebo in adult patients with irritable bowel syndrome, with an adaptive enrichment analysis keyed to baseline mycobiome composition. It is deposited as a timestamped, citable, publicly available record of the trial design prior to execution by any investigator or sponsor. 
 The protocol specifies the design rationale, population, intervention, endpoints, randomization and blinding procedures, statistical analysis plan (including interim futility analysis and adaptive enrichment methodology), safety monitoring, ethical considerations, data management and sharing commitments, cost estimate, and attribution requirements for adaptation. A pre-registered Phase 3 follow-on architecture is included, with explicit trigger conditions based on Phase 2 outcomes. 
 The trial design tests whether oral fluconazole, at 150 mg daily for 6 weeks, reduces IBS symptom severity in the general adult IBS population, and whether baseline mycobiome characteristics predict treatment response. The primary endpoint is change in IBS Severity Scoring System (IBS-SSS) total score at Week 6, with a 12-week follow-up period to assess durability. A urinary electrolyte panel is included as a pre-specified mechanistic exploratory endpoint. 
 The scientific rationale for the trial is documented in the companion article: Craddock, J. (2026). Clear Evidence, No Trial — Mycobiome IBS and Antifungals. Redacted Science Research Initiative.  https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19645403  
 The protocol is released under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license. Any investigator, institution, or sponsor may adopt, adapt, or execute this design, subject to citation of this document and the companion article. This deposit does not constitute an IND application, an IRB submission, or a clinical trial registry entry; those steps remain the responsibility of any sponsor or investigator electing to execute the trial. 
 This is the first pre-registered protocol in the Clear Evidence, No Trial series from the Redacted Science Research Initiative. The series documents clinical research questions where mechanistic evidence is established, the intervention is specific and available, and the required randomized trial has not been conducted, accompanied where feasible by a pre-registered protocol establishing a citable, timestamped record of the trial design.
